business_summary: Eli Lilly is a global pharmaceutical company that discovers, makes, and sells branded prescription medicines. It makes money by selling patent-protected drugs at high margins, with revenue currently dominated by its incretin (GLP-1/GIP) franchise: tirzepatide, sold as Mounjaro for type-2 diabetes and Zepbound for obesity. Other meaningful products include Verzenio (breast cancer), Jardiance (diabetes/heart, partnered with Boehringer Ingelheim), Taltz (immunology), Trulicity (older diabetes GLP-1), and a legacy insulin/diabetes-care portfolio. The thesis driver is the obesity/diabetes incretin wave: a very large, under-treated patient population, where Lilly and Novo Nordisk are the two clear leaders. Lilly is now scaling an oral GLP-1 (orforglipron) that could broaden access well beyond injectables. The business wins over 5-10 years if it keeps converting its clinical lead and manufacturing build-out into durable, patent-protected cash flows while replenishing the pipeline ahead of patent expiries.

moat.type: Patents/IP on tirzepatide and pipeline molecules, multi-year manufacturing capacity lead in incretins, deep clinical/R&D capability, trusted brand with physicians and payers, and high regulatory barriers to entry

valuation.method: Blend of forward P/E and TTM FCF-yield. P/E uses FY2026 non-GAAP EPS guidance midpoint of $36.25 at 26x/32x/40x (premium multiples justified by ~25%+ revenue growth, 80%+ gross margin and a wide moat). FCF-yield uses TTM FCF/share of ~$13.20 at 2.2%/1.6%/1.2% yields; note FCF currently lags earnings because Lilly is investing heavily in capex to scale manufacturing, so the FCF method intentionally drags the band lower for humility. The base of ~$992 sits below the current $1,206.57 price: the stock is priced for continued exceptional growth and offers little or negative margin of safety today.

bull_case: Incretin franchise (Mounjaro + Zepbound) is the dominant growth engine: Q1 2026 revenue grew 56% YoY to $19.8B, with Zepbound up 80% YoY to $4,160M.
Exceptional unit economics: 81.3% gross margin (FY2024) and ROIC of 16.4% (FY2024) show real pricing power and efficient capital use.
Rapid earnings ramp: FY2024 net income +102% to $10,590M; Q1 2026 reported EPS $8.26 (+170% YoY); company raised FY2026 guidance to revenue $82-85B and non-GAAP EPS $35.50-$37.00.
Oral GLP-1 orforglipron succeeded in multiple Phase 3 trials and is heading to global regulatory submissions; an oral pill could massively expand the addressable market beyond injectables.
Wide moat from tirzepatide patents, a multi-year manufacturing capacity lead, deep R&D, and trusted brand with physicians and payers.
risks: Valuation is rich: at ~$1,206 the price is above the base fair value of ~$992, leaving little or negative margin of safety. The thesis depends on growth continuing for years to justify the multiple.

Free cash flow is much smaller than reported earnings (FY2024 FCF only $3,760M vs net income $10,590M) because of heavy manufacturing capex; if growth disappoints, the cash-conversion gap becomes a problem.
Concentration: a large and rising share of revenue and growth comes from a single molecule, tirzepatide. Any safety signal, label restriction, or efficacy setback would hit hard.
Intense competition from Novo Nordisk (semaglutide) and a wave of next-gen and oral incretins; pricing pressure is already visible (Q1 2026 lower realized prices on Mounjaro/Zepbound).
U.S. drug-pricing policy, Medicare negotiation, and payer/PBM coverage decisions could compress prices on the franchise.
Patent cliff risk longer term: incretin composition/method patents will eventually expire, exposing the franchise to generics/biosimilars; pipeline must replace them.
Net debt-to-EBITDA of ~2.1x is moderate but not trivial given large ongoing capex commitments.
what_would_break_thesis: Gross margin falls below 70% for two consecutive quarters (signals lost pricing power / commoditization of incretins).
Total revenue declines year-over-year for two consecutive quarters (the growth engine has stalled).
Net debt-to-EBITDA rises above 3.0x (balance sheet stress from capex or M&A).
A material safety signal, FDA label restriction/black-box warning, or recall on tirzepatide (Mounjaro/Zepbound) that meaningfully impairs the franchise.
ROIC falls below 10% on a full-year basis (capital is no longer compounding at a high rate).
Free cash flow turns negative for a full fiscal year without a clear, temporary capex explanation.
A serious accounting red flag or restatement.
catalysts: Orforglipron (oral GLP-1) regulatory decisions: potential U.S. obesity action in 2026 and diabetes/global submissions, which could re-rate the addressable market.
Continued Mounjaro/Zepbound volume growth and new manufacturing capacity coming online to relieve supply constraints.
FY2026 guidance execution: revenue $82-85B and non-GAAP EPS $35.50-$37.00; quarterly beats/raises.
Pipeline readouts and label expansions (e.g., cardiovascular/sleep apnea/MASH indications for tirzepatide) that broaden reimbursement.
New product launches replenishing the portfolio ahead of long-term patent expiries.
